steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best describes the overall structure of the passage. Map the passage's moves: it presents a study and its findings, raises one possible explanation for the results, then rules that explanation out.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A study of park use in Bogotá is described, and then a similar study of park use in Fortaleza is analyzed to corroborate the findings of the Bogotá study.: The passage discusses one study, not two. ✗
  • Choice B — A study involving surveys in Bogotá and Fortaleza is introduced, and then a possible explanation for some of the results is considered and rejected.: This matches exactly — the study and its findings come first, then the possible explanation (varying access to nonpark amenities) is raised and then dismissed. ✓
  • Choice C — An unexpected finding is described, and then that finding is attributed to the method used to collect survey data in Bogotá and Fortaleza.: The passage doesn't criticize the study's methodology. ✗
  • Choice D — An unresolved question about park planning is described, and then examples from a study of Bogotá and Fortaleza are used to answer that question.: The passage doesn't open with an explicit question — it presents findings and tests an explanation. ✗
That is the correct answer.
